attacking_style: Launches long-range attacks (10km+ from finish) from breakaway situations, typically on climbs or transitional terrain. Lacks explosive acceleration for decisive separation, relies on sustained effort and race reading rather than pure speed.
team_tactics: Deployed as early attacking card to soften field before team leader moves, or as high-tempo domestique to pace GC groups in finals. Given stage-hunting freedom when team has no GC pressure.
terrain_preference: Sustained climbs and technical descents where power and bike-handling compensate for acceleration deficit. Vulnerable in situations requiring explosive attacks or sprint finishes after efforts.
vulnerability: Sprint finishes expose critical weakness—repeatedly caught or outkicked after making race-winning efforts. Telegraphed attacks from breakaways allow marking and countering in closing kilometers.
key_patterns: Attacks from breakaways 3-10km out but gets marked/outsprinted (3 instances across 2019-2022)
Functions as early-race disruptor before team leader moves (2 confirmed tactical setups)
Strong descending/bridging ability showcased on technical finales like Selvino descent
High-tempo domestique pacing GC groups in finals when not stage hunting
Exceptional one-day form windows but inconsistent podium threat across season
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 showed persistent attacking intent but same finishing limitations; no progression in conversion rate from strong positioning to victories since 2019 breakthrough
